CAN: construct directed (PyDiGraph) and undirected (PyGraph) graphs efficiently
compute Dijkstra shortest paths and path lengths
find connected components in undirected graphs
handle large graphs with Rust-backed performance
CANNOT: perform learnable message passing (use PyG for GNNs)
represent topological complexes with boundary operators (use toponetx)
prove graph properties formally (use z3 for logical proofs)

pytorch.reason: not used: this probe isolates rustworkx graph algorithm capabilities alone; cross-tool coupling is deferred to a separate integration sim per the four-sim-kinds doctrine (capability vs integration separation).

pyg.reason: PyG provides GNN message passing; rustworkx provides algorithmic graph operations; separate capabilities, no overlap needed here.

betweenness_centrality.detail: Star center has higher betweenness than leaves

no_path_in_disconnected.detail: Disconnected graph: no path from n0 to n1

directed_no_reverse_path.detail: Directed edge a→b: forward path exists, reverse does not

path_to_self.detail: Shortest path from node to itself is trivial
